title: Limit the Number of Returned Documents

sections.heading: Step 1: Specify the limit.
sections.body:
- Select the collection.

- In the Query Bar, click **Options**.

- Enter an integer representing the number of documents to
  return into the **Limit** field.

- Click Find to run the query and view the updated
  results.

![Results of specifying query limit](/atlas-ui/images/atlas-ui/querybar/query-limit-success.png)

sections.heading: Clear the Query
sections.body:
To clear the query bar and the results of the query, click
Reset.

sections.heading: How Does the Atlas Query Compare to MongoDB and SQL Queries?
sections.body:
[$skip](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/skip) corresponds to the `LIMIT ...` clause
in a SQL (Structured Query Language) `SELECT` statement.

> **Example:** You have 3,235 articles. You would like to see a list of the
> first 10 articles.
>
> SQL
>
> ```sql
> SELECT * FROM article
> LIMIT 10;
> ```
>
> MongoDB Aggregation
>
> ```javascript
> db.article.aggregate(
>   { $limit : 10 }
> );
> ```
>
> Atlas
>
>  Limit Option
>
> ```javascript
> $limit : 10
> ```
